behavior_being_monitored: Self-harm (historically, now few incidents), Struggling to get up for school, Refusing to attend education, Vaping in school, Abusive language towards teachers, Multiple school exclusions, Struggling to maintain personal hygiene, Struggling to take medication consistently, Excessive snacking on crisps and chocolate, Struggling to engage in physical activities, Selectivity with food, Making unkind remarks towards the team, Substance misuse, Refusing dental and optical appointments
target_outcome: Building positive and trusting relationships, Opening up about past experiences, Discussing the importance of attending school and routines, Sharing tasks to help with morning routine, Offering incentives for appointments and pocket money, Team role-modelling healthy lifestyle, Tracking steps/progress with a watch, Involving Logan in weekly menu planning, Educating on the importance of a balanced diet, Offering healthier food choices and alternatives, Providing a walking pad, Discussing risks of not performing health checks (e.g., breast checks), Offering stop smoking services and continued discussions, Supporting the reduction of nicotine in vapes, Encouraging consistent morning routine for education, Providing breakfast options, Encouraging a tidy room (with incentives), Reminding Logan of school attendance incentives, Team liaising with school and transport driver, Obtaining school work if not attending, Limiting internet access for non-educational use, Encouraging social activities with friends, Celebrating events/parties at Omega, Regular keywork sessions, Providing a 1:1 support tutor in school, Celebrating positive achievements, Promoting an active lifestyle and good diet for endorphin release

daily_summary: Engage in de-briefs, reflections, and chain analysis after incidents.
recommended_actions: Talking to the team, venting, and reflecting, Engaging in chosen activities, Offering choices of activities, Team spending time in bedroom if requested, Opening up about feelings to CAMHS worker, Discussing the importance of routines and attendance, Exploring different therapies (EMDR, CBT), Debriefs, reflections, and chain analysis after incidents, Help understanding emotions, Researching and discussing new coping strategies. Crisis: Access to the initial response team and crisis team when in crisis. Social worker to be updated on critical situations.

recommended_actions: Reassurance, Familiar routines, Memory aids. Crisis: Not specified

behavior_being_monitored: Unauthorised absences from home, Lack of engagement in college
target_outcome: Empowering the team to promote relationships with Ruby, Implementing rewards for Ruby, Allowing pets (Coco and new kitten) to live at Hebe (with Ruby's care responsibility), Promoting activities and weekend away with Emma (risk assessed), Preparing for supported accommodation through PFAL sessions

Crisis: CAWN (Child's Assessment of Welfare Needs) consideration and processing. Contacting police and recording a concern for welfare if Ruby does not answer the door when staff attempt to collect her.

behavior_being_monitored: Self-harm (superficial cuts, suicide ideation history), Emotional struggles, Substance misuse (vaping, illegal substances, ketamine), Disengagement with team, Directly swearing at others, Threats to go missing or leave home, Raising her voice, Stimming (repeatedly tapping fingers/thumbs), Declining medication, Throwing items (bedroom, lounge), Wearing long sleeve top in hot weather, Pushing boundaries (e.g., e-cig use), Refusing to settle to bed, Shouting/swearing on calls to Mum/Nan, Refusing to hand in phone/iPad (end of day, safety checks), Physical behaviors towards teachers (pushing past, blocking, shouting unkind remarks), Aggression, Property damage (punching walls, damaging items), Absconding (walking out, not contacting, refusing location), Displaying different attitudes with team members (kind/negative), Exaggerating/making up situations, Attempting to create tension with team, Loudness, Calling others names
target_outcome: Celebrate positive achievements, Reinforce positive decisions, Encourage active lifestyle and good diet to reduce stress, Help Logan understand and process emotions, Research and discuss new coping strategies and techniques, Team always available for talk/support, Work with outside agencies and provide updates, Lead by example in promoting healthy lifestyle, Involve Logan in menu planning for balanced diet, Offer incentives for personal hygiene and school attendance, Team engagement in fun activities, Provide resources like Fitbit, walking pad, watch for physical activity tracking
environmental_factors: Family time, Talking to family or friends, Being told 'No', Lack of consistency/blurring boundaries by care team, Being challenged in front of others, Frustration with education or not wanting to attend, Team trying to talk about self-harm, Family members talking about self-harm, Feeling overwhelmed, Influenced by peers/peer pressure, Mum contacting Logan about problems, Being asked to complete unwanted tasks, Being asked to hand in phone/iPad, Bravado, Getting ready for school/makeup/hair not to her liking, Following peers' negative behaviors
daily_summary: After a physical intervention, Logan requires time to recover, with support led by her needs. This includes time to regulate and reflect, offering a hug/chat/activity, and repairing the relationship. The team must ask if she is ready to chat and respect her decision. Helpful strategies include time to rest, making a drink, quiet time with the team or alone in her bedroom, reflection, distraction (if not wanting to talk), support/reassurance, and encouraging a drive or change of environment.
recommended_actions: Redirect self-harm thoughts (watching TV, driving, listening to music), Use agreed phrase 'I want to make a cheesecake' to signal need for support, Offer to spend time with Logan, encourage change of environment, Offer activities to regulate emotions (walk, drive, diamond art, listening to music), Increase supervision (regular welfare checks if alone), Discuss support in Keywork sessions, Offer CAMHS crisis line access, Talk to Logan on a 1:1 basis, Offer time away from others causing distress, Go for a 1:1 walk, Spend time reminding Logan of past achievements, Use humor, Offer a short drive to remove from situation, Allow Logan to walk alone for 30 minutes to reflect, Use Logan's Spotify playlist to regulate emotions, Ask Logan if she would like a hug, Use calm talking paired with active listening, Give Logan time and space to reflect, Implement delayed engagement (5 minutes without engagement) if behaviors are prolonged, allowing for reflection. Crisis: If Logan's life is at risk or presents serious harm, physical intervention following PRICE principles may be used as a last resort. This includes techniques such as single/adapted embrace, side hug, figure of four, straight arm transition, standing/seated double embrace, seated shoulder embrace, and leg support. Force must be proportionate, minimal, and for the shortest time. If de-escalation fails or team cannot keep Logan safe, escalate to GP, out of hours services, Social Care, 999, or A&E.

monitoring_reason: To identify triggers and develop effective coping strategies
target_outcome: Reduce anxiety responses to 1-2 times per week through better planning and coping strategies

incidents.behavior_observed: Became visibly anxious when different staff member arrived
incidents.antecedent: Expected regular carer (James) but Lucy arrived instead without advance notice
incidents.consequence: Danny was shown visual schedule and given time to process. Calmed down after 10 minutes
incidents.intervention_used: Visual schedule reminder, calm explanation, processing time

positive_behaviors.positive_behavior: Successfully completed morning routine independently using checklist
Took medication independently using photo chart without prompting

daily_summary: One moderate anxiety episode due to unexpected staff change. Danny responded well to visual supports and calming strategies. Good recovery and rest of day positive. Recommendation: ensure advance notice of staff changes when possible
pattern_identified: Anxiety triggered by unexpected changes, particularly staff changes. Visual supports and processing time very effective
recommended_actions: Continue to provide advance notice of any changes. Maintain visual schedule. Consider creating staff photo board
